key_insight: The paper introduces the Patnaik-Pearson dimension, a new intrinsic-dimension estimator for data manifolds, and shows it can track how neural-network representations change across layers.
engineering_relevance: This gives practitioners a geometry-based diagnostic for understanding token embeddings and internal representations in transformers, with possible links to model scaling and spectral properties of weights.
method: Builds a new intrinsic-dimension measure inspired by TwoNN, HTSR, and SETOL.
Proves theoretical properties of the estimator and studies its behavior under common neural-network transformations.
Connects the estimator to Pareto-tailed weight spectra and compares it on BERT-base and DeepSeek-R1-Distill-Qwen-1.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports both theoretical analysis and numerical experiments on transformer token embeddings, including layerwise evolution of the estimated intrinsic dimension.
watch_item: Results may depend on the assumed manifold structure and on fitting power-law tails, which can be sensitive to finite-sample effects and implementation choices.
paper_title: Patnaik-Pearson intrinsic dimension for internal representations of neural networks
